entries.definition: Respiratory disease characterized by the rapid onset of widespread inflammation in the lungs.
Immunity acquired during a person’s lifetime upon exposure to an infectious agent either by previous infection or immunization.
Mode of indirect transmission of an infectious disease that occurs when an infectious agent is carried by dust or droplet nuclei suspended in the air in the form of aerosols.

Class of ectoenzymes found in, e.g., the lungs, heart, skin, or blood vessels, whose function is to cleave circulating peptides, among which angiotensinogen, which are then used to regulate specific cell functions.

japanese.term: アンジオテンシン変換酵素

entries.definition: Protein produced by B lymphocytes that can recognize and neutralize an antigen.

entries.term_en: (syn.) basic reproduction ratio, basic reproductive ratio, 𝑅₀
entries.definition: Average number of secondary cases that one case produces in a population at risk.

Liquid part of the blood in which red and white blood cells and platelets are suspended and which carries such blood cells as well as proteins, mineral salts, sugars, fats, and hormones throughout the body.
Proportion of people who die of a given disease out of all the confirmed cases.

entries.term_en: Quinoline that is substituted at position 4 by a [5- (diethylamino)pentan-2-yl]amino group and at position
entries.definition: 7 by chlorine and is used for the treatment of e.g. malaria, lupus erythematosus, and rheumatoid arthritis.
Aggregation of cases of a disease within a given place and time.

Medical imaging technique by which three-dimensional images of the body are obtained by directing a beam of x-rays at the patient and quickly rotating them around the patient’s body, producing cross-sectional images that are then stacked together by a computed tomography scanner.

japanese.term: コンピュータ断層撮影

entries.definition: Case that is confirmed by a laboratory test.
Family of viruses that consist of a core comprising a positive-sense single-stranded RNA, an envelope, a capsid, and spikes.
Infectious disease caused by SARS-CoV-2.

japanese.term: 新型コロナウイルス感染症

entries.definition: Systemic inflammatory response syndrome caused by a large and rapid release of cytokines into the blood, which may be triggered by different factors, e.g. a viral infection or the use of certain drugs in immunotherapy.

japanese.term: サイトカイン放出症候群

chinese.term: 细胞因子释放综合征 death-to-case ratio

entries.definition: Double-stranded nucleic acid whose nucleotides consist of a backbone made up by a modified sugar, called deoxyribose, a nitrogenous base, i.e. adenine, guanine, cytosine, and thymine, and a phosphate group.

Mode of direct transmission of an infectious disease that occurs when a person is in the proximity of an infected person who has respiratory symptoms, e.g., via coughing, sneezing, breathing, or talking.
Strategy adopted by a public body to reduce to zero the incidence of a disease in a given geographical area.
Innate immune response mechanism that increases a person’s body temperature above the normal level.

chinese.term: 发热 (syn.) 发烧 filtering facepiece respirator

entries.definition: Respiratory protective equipment that uses filters to remove hazardous substances from the air being breathed by the wearer.

japanese.term: フィルター式フェイスピースレスピレーター

entries.definition: Personal protective equipment that protects the wearer’s eyes and face from sprays, splashes, and large-particle droplets, and prevents the spread of respiratory droplets from the wearer to others.
Indirect protection from an infectious agent achieved in a population at risk when a sufficiently large proportion of individuals in the population show an acquired immunity to that infectious agent.
The first known case of the outbreak of a disease in a cluster.

chinese.term: 指示病例 indirect contact transmission

entries.definition: Proportion of people who will die of a certain disease out of all those who have been infected.
Hospital facility that provides critical care and life support for acutely ill or injured patients.
Invasive lung ventilation method in which a tube is inserted into the patient’s trachea through the mouth or nose.
Strategy adopted by a public body because of an emergency that requires people to remain in a certain location which they may not leave other than for exceptional reasons.

entries.term_en: (syn.) magnetic resonance tomography, nuclear magnetic resonance imaging, MRI, MRT, NMRI
entries.definition: Medical imaging technique by which three-dimensional images of the body are obtained by exciting protons present in the water of living tissues and detecting the change in the direction of their rotational axis by means of a magnetic resonance imaging scanner.

Machine that helps patients to breathe by driving fresh air into the lungs by positive pressure and/or by pumping expiratory air out of the lungs by negative pressure, replacing some or all of the muscular effort required for lung ventilation.
Means by which an infectious agent spreads from one host to another.
Inability of two or more organs or organ systems to perform their function.

chinese.term: 多器官功能衰竭 nasopharyngeal swab test

entries.definition: Positive pressure ventilation provided through a person’s upper respiratory airways using an oxygen mask.
Epidemic disease that has spread to different countries and continents.
Gear worn by an individual that provides protection against one or more risks to a person’s health and safety.
Method of performing a laboratory test at or near the site of patient care.

synonyms: pruebas a la cabecera del paciente

japanese.term: ポイントオブケアテスティング
synonyms: ポイントオブケアテス

entries.definition: Molecular biology technique that applies high temperature and a DNA polymerase to a sample of DNA containing the target sequence, thereby producing copies of small segments of the original DNA for use in further molecular and genetic analysis.

spanish.term: reacción en cadena de la polimerasa

synonyms: réaction en chaîne à la polymérase

japanese.term: ポリメラーゼ連鎖反応

entries.definition: Case to whom the onset of the outbreak of a disease can be traced back.
Reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction in which detection and quantification of amplified DNA sequences are performed after each PCR cycle.

spanish.term: reacción en cadena de la polimerasa con transcripción inversa en tiempo real

japanese.term: リアルタイム逆転写ポリメラーゼ連鎖反応

portuguese.term: reação em cadeia da polimerase da transcrição reversa em tempo real

russian.term: полимеразная цепная реакция с обратной транскрипцией в реальном времени

chinese.term: 实时逆转录聚合酶链反应

entries.definition: Particle made of water, proteins, viral RNA, and non- volatile metabolites, which is >5-10 μm in diameter and is exhaled from the nose and mouth of a person whilst e.g. coughing, sneezing breathing, or talking.
Set of practices and behaviours adopted by an individual with the aim of preventing the spread of respiratory diseases.

synonyms: 咳嗽礼仪 respiratory protective equipment

entries.definition: Personal protective equipment that protects the wearer against inhalation of hazardous substances in the air, such as dust, fumes, gases, or biological substances.

chinese.term: 呼吸防护装备 risk factor

entries.definition: Single-stranded nucleic acid whose nucleotides consist of a backbone made up by a simple sugar, called ribose, a phosphate group, and a nitrogenous base, i.e. adenine, guanine, cytosine, and uracil.
Measure taken by an individual in order to avoid contact with other people and thus help to prevent the spread of an infectious disease.
Laboratory test aimed at detecting in the blood serum of an individual the presence of antibodies produced in response to a given disease.

chinese.term: 血清学检测 severe acute respiratory syndrome

entries.definition: Infectious disease caused by SARS-CoV-1.

japanese.term: 重症急性呼吸器症候群

chinese.term: 严重急性呼吸系统综合征
synonyms: SARS severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2

entries.definition: Coronavirus that causes COVID-19.

spanish.term: coronavirus del síndrome respiratorio agudo grave 2
synonyms: coronavirus 2 del síndrome respiratorio agudo grave

portuguese.term: coronavírus da síndrome respiratória aguda grave 2
synonyms: coronavírus 2 da síndrome respiratória aguda grave

russian.term: второй коронавирус тяжелого острого респираторного синдрома

chinese.term: 严重急性呼吸综合征冠状病毒 2

entries.definition: Glycoprotein complex made up of three protomers and located on the envelope of coronaviruses, which enables the virus to bind to the receptor site of the host cells.

japanese.term: スパイク糖タンパク質

entries.definition: Condition characterized by a disproportionate inflammatory response to a variety of severe clinical phenomena, including relating to body temperature (>38°C or <36°C), heart rate (>90 beats per minute), respiratory rate (>20 breaths per minute or PaCO2 <32 mmHg), and white blood cell count (>12,000/cu mm, <4,000/cu mm, or >10% immature band forms).

japanese.term: 全身性炎症反応症候群

entries.definition: A walk-in clinic led by qualified medical professionals, i.e. doctors and nurses, in which urgent care is delivered.
Image formed by the shadows of the objects inside a patient’s body when x-rays travel through it and pass through an x-ray detector on the other side of the patient.
